name: Scrapy
what_it_does: High-performance Python web scraping and crawling framework. Scrapy provides a complete spider lifecycle (request scheduling, downloading, parsing, pipeline processing) with async I/O via Twisted. Built-in support for robots.txt, rate limiting, cookie handling, caching, and item pipelines for storing scraped data. The de-facto standard for large-scale Python web scraping.
use_cases: Build agent data collection pipelines that crawl entire websites extracting structured data using CSS/XPath selectors
Schedule and run automated web scrapers that feed agent knowledge bases with regularly updated content
Extract product data, prices, and inventory from e-commerce sites for agent competitive intelligence
Crawl documentation sites to build agent-searchable knowledge stores from HTML content
Run large-scale domain crawls with rate limiting, politeness rules, and resume support for agent training data
not_for: JavaScript-heavy single-page applications — Scrapy doesn't execute JS; use Playwright or Selenium for SPAs
Simple one-off data extractions — requests + BeautifulSoup is simpler for small tasks
Real-time event-driven scraping — Scrapy is batch-oriented; use streaming solutions for real-time needs
best_when: You need to crawl many pages systematically with built-in rate limiting, pipelines, and middleware for production-grade web scraping in Python.
avoid_when: The target site requires JavaScript rendering or you need a simple one-off scrape — use Playwright or requests+BeautifulSoup respectively.

auth.notes: Scrapy is a self-hosted Python framework with no authentication model of its own. Handles auth on target sites via cookies, headers, or form login via spiders.

pricing.paid_tiers: Scrapy Cloud (Zyte managed hosting) available separately
pricing.notes: Scrapy framework itself is free and open source. Zyte (formerly Scrapinghub) offers managed Scrapy Cloud hosting and anti-bot proxy services commercially.

performance.rate_limits: Self-configured: DOWNLOAD_DELAY, AUTOTHROTTLE, CONCURRENT_REQUESTS settings control rate

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Detailed logging via Python logging module. Scrapy stats (DOWNLOADER/SPIDER errors, retry counts) available in stats collector. Item pipeline errors propagate cleanly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Scrapy has a built-in HTTP cache middleware (HTTPCACHE_ENABLED) for idempotent re-runs. Without cache, spiders re-crawl all URLs on each run.
known_agent_gotchas: Scrapy runs on Twisted async I/O — mixing with asyncio requires scrapy-asyncio bridge or Python 3.10+ native asyncio reactor support
JavaScript-rendered content is not accessible without scrapy-playwright or scrapy-splash middleware — many modern sites require JS execution
robots.txt is respected by default (ROBOTSTXT_OBEY=True) — agents must disable this setting if scraping non-public content that is allowed
Scrapy uses a global Item pipeline pattern — scraped items flow through all pipelines; ordering matters and errors in one pipeline can drop items silently
Memory usage can grow with large crawls if DEPTH_LIMIT and URL deduplication are not tuned — monitor DUPEFILTER stats to detect loops
Anti-bot detection (Cloudflare, Akamai) blocks naive Scrapy requests — requires rotating proxies, browser fingerprint emulation, or Zyte Smart Proxy Manager

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted framework — security model depends on deployment. Twisted handles TLS. Scrapy settings file must not contain credentials in version control.
